source.title: Computational Design of Enantiocomplementary Epoxide Hydrolases for Asymmetric Synthesis of Aliphatic and Aromatic Diols

initial_request.research_problem: Directed evolution for enantioselective enzymes requires extensive high-throughput screening, which is time-consuming and expensive.
initial_request.target_object: Enantiocomplementary epoxide hydrolases for asymmetric synthesis of aliphatic and aromatic diols from meso-epoxides
initial_request.known_context: Previous work showed that computational design using the CASCO framework (Rosetta + MD) can reduce library size and predict enantioselectivity, enabling larger jumps in sequence space.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Directed evolution for enantioselective enzymes requires extensive high-throughput screening, which is time-consuming and expensive.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can computational design reduce library size and predict enantioselectivity, enabling larger jumps in sequence space?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Computational design using the CASCO framework (Rosetta + MD) can reduce library size and predict enantioselectivity, enabling larger jumps in sequence space.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Adopt CASCO framework for design and screening.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Multiple mutations can be explored simultaneously, allowing for larger jumps in sequence space than directed evolution and structure-based rational mutagenesis. In silico screening of enzyme variants by docking and high-throughput molecular dynamics simulations makes it possible to predict enzyme properties and decrease library size for experimental evaluation from thousands to dozens.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to design strategy targeting active-site positions.

evidence.quote_or_span: Multiple mutations can be explored simultaneously, allowing for larger jumps in sequence space than directed evolution and structure-based rational mutagenesis.

in silico screening of enzyme variants by docking and high-throughput molecular dynamics simulations makes it possible to predict enzyme properties and decrease library size for experimental evaluation from thousands to dozens.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that CASCO framework can reduce library size and predict enantioselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which positions and residues should be targeted to achieve enantiocomplementarity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Targeting 11 active-site positions randomized to 9 hydrophobic residues will generate designs with proRR or proSS attack.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design strategy: target 11 positions around the active site, randomize to 9 hydrophobic residues (AFGILMPVW).
evolution_trajectory.observation: The explored sequence space was created by targeting 11 selected positions around the active site with randomization to any of the nine hydrophobic residues (AFGILMPVW).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Subject designed variants to MD screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: The explored sequence space was created by targeting 11 selected positions around the active site (Figure 1B) with randomization to any of the nine hydrophobic residues (AFGILMPVW).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which designs have high predicted enantioselectivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: MD simulations with NAC scoring can identify designs with high enantioselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform HTMI-MD screening of 15,000 designs with at least 5×10 ps simulations; top designs receive up to 80×10 ps and 5×100 ps. Score NAC% on-the-fly.
evolution_trajectory.observation: For each of the 15000 Rosetta designs, at least five independently initialized MD runs of 10 ps were performed. Designs that passed initial selection rounds were subjected to more MD simulations, up to 80×10 ps and 5×100 ps. For each designed new variant 20–80 independently started MD simulations of 10 ps were used.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze MD results; stilbene oxide designs show higher NAC frequencies.

evidence.quote_or_span: For each of the 15000 Rosetta designs, at least five independently initialized MD runs of 10 ps were performed (Table 1). Designs that passed initial selection rounds were subjected to more MD simulations, up to 80×10 ps and 5×100 ps.
For each designed new variant 20–80 independently started MD simulations of 10 ps were used (previously maximally 20 MD simulations).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do different substrates yield different prediction accuracies?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Bulkier substrates like stilbene oxide will have higher predicted enantioselectivities.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare NAC frequencies and predicted enantioselectivities across substrates.
evolution_trajectory.observation: A larger fraction of the stilbene oxide designs displayed high NAC frequencies and high predicted enantioselectivities than what was found with cyclopentene and butene oxide designs.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Despite high NAC frequencies, visual inspection reveals structural problems in stilbene oxide designs.

evidence.quote_or_span: a larger fraction of the stilbene oxide designs displayed high NAC frequencies and high predicted enantioselectivities than what was found with cyclopentene and butene oxide designs.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Stilbene oxide designs show high NAC frequencies.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Are the high NAC frequencies reliable?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Visual inspection may reveal structural problems not captured by NAC scoring.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 10 of the 20 inspected variants for stilbene oxide displayed structural problems.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to experimental testing of top designs from MD screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: 10 of the 20 inspected variants for stilbene oxide displayed structural problems.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Top designs selected from MD screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do the designed variants exhibit the predicted enantioselectivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The designed variants will show the enantioselectivity predicted by MD simulations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct and test 28 new LEH variants experimentally.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Chiral analysis of the formed diols revealed that 21 (77%) of the designed active variants showed the enantioselectivity predicted by MD simulations (10% ee cutoff, Table 2).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze results per substrate; note prediction failures for stilbene oxide.

evidence.quote_or_span: Chiral analysis of the formed diols revealed that 21 (77%) of the designed active variants showed the enantioselectivity predicted by MD simulations (10% ee cutoff, Table 2).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Experimental results obtained for all substrates.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How accurate are predictions for small aliphatic substrates?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Predictions are more accurate for small aliphatic substrates than for aromatic stilbene oxide.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze prediction accuracy for cyclopentene oxide and butene oxide.
evolution_trajectory.observation: For cyclopentene oxide (1a): 9/10 active, all predicted correctly; proSS designs gave higher ee (56–85%) than proRR. For butene oxide (2a): 10/10 active, 8/10 predicted correctly; proSS better (best 77% ee).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Despite good predictions for small substrates, stilbene oxide shows failures.

evidence.quote_or_span: Indeed, designs for substrate 1a and 2a carrying both L35W and I116V gave an (S,S)-diol preference of >73% ee (Table 2).
side are accompanied by I116V, leading to (S,S)-butanediol formation with the best ee of 77%.

In case of cyclopentene oxide 1a, directed evolution $^{[47,50-52]}$ and previous computational design $^{[32]}$ gave $(S,S)$ -selective LEH variants producing the diol with similar ee as found here (60–95% ee, Table 2).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why do stilbene oxide predictions fail?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Stilbene oxide predictions are less accurate due to π-π interactions not captured by MD.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze prediction failures for stilbene oxide.
evolution_trajectory.observation: For stilbene oxide (3a): 6/8 active predicted correctly; two mismatches, including variant 52A predicted (R,R) but gave (S,S) with 97% ee. Four enantioselectivities opposite to Rosetta design target; MD corrected two.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Despite failures, high enantioselectivities achieved for stilbene oxide.

evidence.quote_or_span: four of the observed enantioselectivities with stilbene oxide 3a were opposite to the Rosetta design target
The stilbene oxide enantioselectivities of designs 41B and 52A are higher than reported for other LEH variants tested on this substrate.

the two most $(S,S)$ -diol selective mutants for 3a (52A, 41B) carried mutation I116F, a mutation that introduces steric hindrance at the central side and in case of substrates 1a and 2a favors $(R,R)$ -selectivity
Whereas most designs produced $(R,R)$ -diol 3b (with ee >99% for two variants), enantiocomplementary mutants yielding $(S,S)$ -3b were also found, with a highest ee of 97%.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Prediction failures observed for stilbene oxide.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can high enantioselectivities still be obtained for stilbene oxide?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some designed variants will exhibit high enantioselectivity for stilbene oxide.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Identify best variants for stilbene oxide.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The best enantioselectivities were clearly obtained with stilbene oxide 3a. Whereas most designs produced (R,R)-diol 3b (with ee >99% for two variants), enantiocomplementary mutants yielding (S,S)-3b were also found, with a highest ee of 97%.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test best variants at preparative scale.

evidence.quote_or_span: the best enantioselectivities were clearly obtained with stilbene oxide 3a
Whereas most designs produced $(R,R)$ -diol 3b (with ee >99% for two variants), enantiocomplementary mutants yielding $(S,S)$ -3b were also found, with a highest ee of 97%.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Best variants identified for stilbene oxide.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can these variants be used for preparative scale conversions?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants 60A and 41B will enable preparative scale synthesis of enantiopure diols.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform preparative scale conversions of cis-stilbene oxide using variants 60A and 41B under optimized conditions (10% and 15% dioxane, 40°C).
evolution_trajectory.observation: The best conditions (10% dioxane and 40°C) were combined and gave a conversion of 86 and 63% for variants 60A and 41B, respectively. Increasing the dioxane concentration to 15% was beneficial for the proRR variant 60A, yielding a conversion of 98%. The conversion of cis-stilbene oxide by variant 60A was further improved (63 to 80%) by increasing the reaction temperature from 30 to 40°C.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze kinetic properties to understand activity changes.

evidence.quote_or_span: The best conditions (10% dioxane and 40°C) were combined and gave a conversion of 86 and 63% for variants 60A and 41B, respectively.
Increasing the dioxane concentration to 15% was beneficial for the proRR variant 60A, yielding a conversion of 98%

Reaction mixtures (total volume 1 mL) contained 10 mg of cis-stilbene oxide (final concentration 50 mm, suspension) and 6.37 mg of enzyme (final concentration 320 μm) in 50 mm HEPES, pH 8.0, and were incubated (at 30 or 40°C, 135 rpm) for 48 h.
The conversion of cis-stilbene oxide by variant 60A was further improved (63 to 80%) by increasing the reaction temperature from 30 to 40°C.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why do most designs have lower catalytic activities?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Lower activities are due to higher KM, not lower kcat.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform kinetic analysis on selected variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The main cause of the lower catalytic activities of most designs found during initial tests is due to a higher KM, not a lower kcat. The observation that kcat values are lowered in the designs for 3a whereas NAC percentages during MD simulations were fine indicates that prediction of reactivities across different substrates using such short MD simulations is troublesome. The 3-fold lower kcat of mutant 60A with 3a was accompanied by a 6-fold better KM, leading to an improved catalytic efficiency in 60A.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assess overall prediction accuracy and limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: the main cause of the lower catalytic activities of most designs found during initial tests (Table 2) is due to a higher $K_{M}$ , not a lower $k_{cat}$

The observation that $k_{cat}$ values are lowered in the designs for 3a whereas NAC percentages during MD simulations (Table 2) were fine indicates that prediction of reactivities across different substrates using such short MD simulations is troublesome.
the 3-fold lower $k_{cat}$ of mutant 60A with 3a was accompanied by a 6-fold better $K_{M}$ , leading to an improved catalytic efficiency in 60A.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does prediction accuracy vary between aliphatic and aromatic substrates?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Computational design and MD simulations predict enantioselectivity well for small aliphatic substrates but less accurately for aromatic substrates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude that prediction is better for aliphatic substrates and suggest improved scoring for π-π interactions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Computational design and MD simulations are well able to predict and screen enantioselectivity of LEH variants in case of small aliphatic substrates. Whereas highly selective variants for production of aromatic diols can be obtained, prediction accuracy is lower.

evidence.quote_or_span: computational design and MD simulations are well able to predict and screen enantioselectivity of LEH variants in case of small aliphatic substrates
Whereas highly selective variants for production of aromatic diols can be obtained, prediction accuracy is lower

success_verification.final_conclusion: Computational design and MD simulations predict enantioselectivity well for small aliphatic substrates but less accurately for aromatic substrates. Need improved scoring for π-π interactions.
limitations: MD-based NAC scoring provides qualitative but not quantitative prediction of enantioselectivity
No correlation between NAC% and activity
MD does not account for energy barriers
Prediction accuracy lower for aromatic substrates
